There’s a distinct focus on your inner world and past matters in March, dear Aries, but you’re heading into a power period for activity and involvement. Venus spends the 6th to 30th in Aries, boosting the love and pleasure factors in your life. As such, although you're keeping to yourself in many ways, it’s not limiting your personal appeal! Others are drawn to you more than usual--you are the desired one right now.

The Full Moon on the 3rd is a Lunar Eclipse, and it brings some chaos on the job or daily front, but nothing you can’t handle. This eclipse reminds you of the need to find a work-rest balance. It calls you to action, mainly to take better care of daily routines, wellness, and work matters. Seeing the flaws in current systems can be irritating, but can lead to significant improvements.

March brings plenty of opportunities to explore your social and happiness goals, dear Taurus. It’s a time of interesting developments with friends, group activities, collaboration, networking, and attention to long-term projects. While demands on your time can often be quite pleasant, you do need to pay particular attention to managing your time effectively with Mercury retrograde until the 20th. Delays and backtracking can be part of the picture. The New Moon on the 18th brings a fresh start with friends.

Friendships might be renewed or revitalized, even with (and possibly because) there are hurdles to clear and issues to resolve. While the month finds you in mostly good spirits, there are some key times to watch. Expect the Lunar Eclipse to bring some level of mystery to the first week of March. It occurs on the 3rd, and it brings emotions to the surface that are valid, but information coming to light now may not be complete. It’s best to reserve judgment.

In March, your focus continues on your career, reputation, status, life direction, and responsibilities to the outside world, dear Gemini. Influences call your attention to your long-range goals and ambitions. But while March can be a higher-profile period, you can find beautiful ways to detach and detox. Friendship, happiness goals, and your special projects and plans are in good shape this month.

This month is strong for new approaches in your career, reputation, networking, friendships, as well as education, sharing or publishing, and travel. The New Moon on the 18th brings fresh energy for career and reputation matters, although it’s better to wait until after the 20th to launch new projects since Mercury is retrograde until then. It's also a time when inspiration comes suddenly, and you could pick up fresh, and possibly radically different, interests or goals.

The Lunar Eclipse on the 3rd is designed to awaken your true feelings about a matter, but there's so much noise surrounding it that it's best to take your time with your revelations before acting on them. This eclipse ultimately magnifies flaws with your living conditions or arrangements, personal life, emotional world, home, or family. Finding a better balance between your professional and personal life is an ongoing theme in your life, and this lunation furthers things along. Family matters or personal needs that have been ignored or brushed aside can erupt now for special consideration.

March brings great vibes for exploring and discovering, dear Cancer, particularly different ideas, places, and experiences. You have a strong showing of planets in your solar ninth house, pulling up themes of learning, non-routine activities, sharing ideas, publishing, connecting with new people, expanding your interests and mind, and travel. March is a month for being more experimental, spontaneous, and adventurous than usual. The need to “get away from it all” is compelling. You’re looking for spirit-lifting activities! Still, a growing theme this month is about work, responsibilities, and long-term goals.

But first, the Full Moon on the 3rd is a Lunar Eclipse, and it can bring quite a scurry of activity in the surrounding weeks. It happens when Mercury is already retrograde, and delayed travel or issues with communication and transportation systems in your life can wreak some havoc, although this is nothing you can’t fix. In fact, you may be better off for it. Aim to refine things rather than push something ahead. Fortunately, there can be important discoveries, ideas, and changes in your mindset or attitude. Watch for impulsive self-expression, but give your emotions and epiphanies weight. Recent inattention to details or daily affairs may need to be rectified.

Pay particular attention to new information and ideas coming to you from the 4th to the 8th, when there can be a new understanding of past matters that can eventually fuel successful new plans and beginnings. The conversations you have now can be inspiring and enlightening.

The month’s energies are natural for looking within, dear Leo. Finances, mainly shared money and property, loans, and support, deserve special attention. A passion project or special relationship can consume much of your attention. You’re in good shape to get in better touch with your inner power and recognize or build upon support from others. Opportunities to pool your resources with someone can emerge. Exploring new ideas is also in focus, particularly as March progresses.

March is powerful for balance, harmony, and partnerships, dear Virgo, as well as for research and review. You’re finding a better balance, solving problems, and enjoying companionship this month. You're often focused on your relationships and gaining a different perspective on your life. It’s the time of year when others’ agendas and needs tend to come into the spotlight.

There can be new beginnings for an alliance after the New Moon on the 18th, and the 20th brings more forward-looking energy to a relationship. There is a strong focus on partnering, socializing, and getting along with others this month. Financial or intimate matters take on more importance as the month progresses.

A strong focus on your wellness and work routines continues in March and gains further traction, dear Libra, but your social life is gaining your attention. It's a time to get your daily affairs and self-care matters into order, and good energy is with you for making adjustments, such as editing or revising.

With your work, methods, habits, or health, you’re likely to be dealing with some backtracking or delays while Mercury is retrograde in your solar sixth house until the 20th. However, overall, the month is strong for enjoying your routines and daily life.

A busy month is pretty much a given, but the Lunar Eclipse on the 3rd can prompt strong feelings, particularly about your need for downtime. It’s important to pay particular attention to your work and chores this month. Still, if you’re overdoing this and failing to take care of your need for mental or emotional refreshment, you’ll get a reminder around this time. Arriving at a balance between work and rest is a challenge, but it’s within reach, and it can be rewarding.

Opportunities to enjoy and express yourself emerge in March, dear Scorpio. Self-expression through hobbies, recreation, love, and leisure is in focus this month. It's a time to share your affection, joy, and experiences. You also gravitate toward playful, celebratory activities. Even with some related delays or backtracking, due to Mercury's retrograde until the 20th, it’s an excellent time to enjoy yourself.

With a strong focus on your joy sector, it can be a playful period–you’re discovering your needs for pleasure, entertainment, and positive feedback. Your self-discovery process can be remarkable in March. Hobbies, recreation, love, and pleasure are in focus. You're also in an excellent place to get ahead with your tasks, work, chores, and health pursuits. Opportunities can arise from special attention to your duties.

During the retrograde Mercury cycle until the 20th, you may find ways to reconnect with activities that nourish your spirit and encourage expressing yourself more freely. There can be a waiting game, delay, stalling, or catch-up period now, but giving due attention to what you’ve overlooked means you end up ahead of the game, ultimately. It would be wise to go easy on speculation and spending at this time. But while some things remain up in the air, on a higher level, you’re feeling a little more directed and confident that you’ll find the right path.

Much energy centers around the home or with family this month, dear Sagittarius. Surrounding yourself with familiar faces and places comes naturally. You require some anchoring and centering! With Mercury retrograde until the 20th, you’ll deal with minor problems and delays, which can undoubtedly slow you down, but pacing yourself should be relatively straightforward.

March is strong for making memories with family or for getting some much-needed downtime. Domestic life may not always run smoothly, but this is an excellent time for making yourself more comfortable. Later in the month, a shift of focus to more creative, sociable, and pleasure-seeking activities occurs.

March brings a strong focus on connecting, learning, transportation, communications, and daily affairs, dear Capricorn. While there can be ups and downs and delays to manage, you’ll ultimately get to see which of your projects are genuinely viable and worth your effort. You’re also likely to enjoy your home and personal life more fully this month. It’s a highly expressive period, even with Mercury retrograde until the 20th. A Lunar Eclipse can prompt cravings for bigger things or some distance from the mundane affairs of your life, and you’ll seek some escapes from time to time.

Mercury's retrograde cycle, lasting until March 20th, occurs in your communications sector and can signal delays and slowdowns. Still, the workarounds you find as a result of mishaps can end up helping you or turning your attention to things you’ve overlooked. Your words may not be perfect, but knowing this, your manner and charm can make up for it.

March brings strong energy for enjoying yourself and building something important to you, dear Aquarius. The desire to boost your income or take care of your things is with you. As long as you avoid impulse buys, you should come out ahead! Your desire to settle in and focus on building, collecting, and getting comfortable is strong. It’s a month to get your bearings, with an emphasis on your sector of resources. You are encouraged to slow down, work things out, draw on patience, and build and develop special projects. The better you accept this pace, the greater the rewards! Still, it can also be a pleasant time for learning and connecting as the month advances.

With Mercury retrograde until the 20th and the Lunar Eclipse on the 3rd, it’s important to focus on developing what you have rather than pushing forward with brand-new beginnings. Fortunately, your astrology encourages a take-it-easy approach this month, and it’s well-timed– you’ll find it more natural than most other signs to slow things down.

The month ahead is a strong one for you, dear Pisces, particularly regarding personal influence and finances. The New Moon on the 18th launches a period of increased personal power and charisma, and on the 20th, Mercury ends its retrograde cycle in your sign. These events give you the green light to move personal projects forward.

While two outer planets have officially left Pisces, you have a big showing of planets and lights in your sign in March. True, Mercury is retrograde for close to three weeks in your sign (until the 20th). However, it gives your emotions and feelings the chance to catch up with your mind, which is often ahead of the pack. Huge insights and the beginnings of significant new ideas can emerge now.

Venus is in Pisces only until the 4th, adding loads to your charm, and the Sun is in your sign until the 20th, increasing your personal influence and presence. Mars heads into Pisces on the 2nd, enlivening your month and stirring your spirit for action. Mercury does help you as well. While you may be putting your foot in your mouth from time to time, you’re also working on clarifying your thoughts and ideas, and you’re getting plenty of room to do so. Take care of unfinished business so that you can begin anew with less baggage. By April, you’ll have a keen sense of what you want and need.
